The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine, offering various health benefits, including anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which can help alleviate symptoms of arthritis and other inflammatory conditions. The bioactive constituents of Hydrochorea elegans include flavonoids, alkaloids, and phenolic compounds, which contribute to its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made with the Brazil nut tree include teas, capsules, and extracts, often used to support immune function and reduce oxidative stress.
